headlines.title: Iran chose 'not to accept our terms', Vance says after negotiations
headlines.description: The US vice-president made the announcement after 21 hours of negotiations in Islamabad, Pakistan.

headlines.title: More than 500 arrests at Palestine Action protest
headlines.description: Event organisers said it would show the "resistance" to the ban on the group was "stronger than ever".

headlines.title: Faisal Islam: Why the government is relaxed about Chinese car imports
headlines.description: The UK government believes the rise of China's car industry could be good for UK consumers and industry.

headlines.title: We spoke to the man making viral Lego-style AI videos for Iran. Experts say it's powerful propaganda
headlines.description: "Slopaganda" is too weak a term to capture how powerful this "highly sophisticated" content is, one expert says.

headlines.title: Asha Bhosle: The sound of Bollywood dies aged 92
headlines.description: Her infectious voice got fans dancing and singing, becoming the soundtrack for generations of Indians.

headlines.title: From blast off to splashdown: My days following Nasa's historic mission to the Moon
headlines.description: BBC Science Editor Rebecca Morelle reflects on how it felt to watch history being made.

headlines.title: Balamory is back - Miss Hoolie and PC Plum lift the lid on what to expect
headlines.description: The cast of the rebooted children's TV hit tell us the new series is a 'love letter to the original'.

headlines.title: I've been a sex educator for six years. Why did I start doubting my contraception choices?
headlines.description: Misinformation about contraception has been spreading on social media, alongside the "very real frustrations" of women complaining about side effects.

headlines.title: The prophet and the mysterious death of Charmain Speirs
headlines.description: A BBC Disclosure investigation has uncovered significant questions about what happened at the hotel where Charmain died.

headlines.title: Hungarians vote in big numbers on whether to end Orbán rule and elect rival
headlines.description: Most polls favour Péter Magyar, who fronts a grassroots party, and early figures point to a record turnout of voters.

headlines.title: The Papers: 'Le Humiliation' and 'US and Iran start historic peace talks'
headlines.description: Some of the UK papers today follow Sir Keir Starmer shelving his Chagos Islands deal, and the historic Iran-US peace talks in Pakistan.

headlines.title: 'We need real peace': Easter truce fails to lift grim mood in war-torn Ukraine
headlines.description: Easter festivities are muted in Kharkiv as Ukrainians expect fighting to flare up again after a weekend truce.

headlines.title: 'It's a special thing to be on Planet Earth': Artemis crew welcomed home in Houston
headlines.description: The four astronauts flew around the Moon in a nine-day voyage that took them further from Earth than any humans in history.

headlines.title: Messy and unpredictable: What I learned from election tour of the UK
headlines.description: BBC Radio 5 Live's Matt Chorley talks to voters in England, Scotland and Wales to get the real story on May's elections.

headlines.title: Golden eagles' return to English skies gets government backing
headlines.description: The birds could be reintroduced as early as next year following a £1m injection from the government.

headlines.title: McIlroy can't stop riding Augusta rollercoaster as final Masters round beckons
headlines.description: Rory McIlroy says he will have to play better on Sunday if he is to defend his Masters title after letting slip a six-shot lead at Augusta National.

headlines.title: I'm the only medic on the island – but I wish I'd brought some trousers
headlines.description: Tammy Hine says she wanted to live somewhere remote and have one last adventure before turning 50.

headlines.title: Girl's 'mammoth' bone find may be 500,000 years old
headlines.description: A scientist says the discovery by seven-year-old Nina Evans could possibly come from a mammoth.

headlines.title: Couple hitchhike to their wedding ceremony
headlines.description: Chloe and George Gardner were driven to their ceremony by a stranger passing by.

headlines.title: The construction boss who built a new life after three years in prison
headlines.description: Traci Quinn, who was jailed for a drugs offence, has transformed herself and set up a successful firm.

headlines.title: BBC News app
headlines.description: Top stories, breaking news, live reporting, and follow news topics that match your interests

headlines.title: Artemis II: Return to the Moon
headlines.description: Four astronauts. One historic mission. Venturing further from Earth than ever before.

headlines.title: US-Iran Peace Talks + Artemis II Returns
headlines.description: It’s a historic day.

headlines.title: Unpack all the latest drama from Race Across the World
headlines.description: Celebrity superfans and travel expert Alfie Watts join Tyler West to discuss the episode!

headlines.title: How young men's lives are influenced by the manosphere
headlines.description: James Blake enters the manosphere, joining men seeking status, wealth and relationships.

headlines.title: Juddering McIlroy simply can't stop riding Masters rollercoaster
headlines.description: Rory McIlroy says he will have to play better on Sunday if he is to defend his Masters title after letting slip a six-shot lead at Augusta National.

headlines.title: The decade-long struggle to get AJ & Fury together
headlines.description: Tyson Fury and Anthony Joshua shared a moment on Saturday, but getting the British heavyweights together this year may not be as straightforward as many suspected.

headlines.title: 'Big punch in the face' - could Arsenal really blow title from here?
headlines.description: Nine points clear with six games to play - could Arsenal still really blow the Premier League title from that position?

headlines.title: England far from perfect but Red Roses machine marches on
headlines.description: England star Ellie Kildunne says a record Women's Six Nations crowd of 77,120 at Allianz Stadium against Ireland is now becoming "the norm".

headlines.title: 'The older I get, the more I enjoy it' - Mullins etches name in Aintree folklore
headlines.description: It is a Grand National to savour for Irish trainer Willie Mullins as he celebrates his third consecutive victory in the race at Aintree - equalling the record set by legendary trainer Vincent O'Brien.

headlines.title: What is going on with Garnacho?
headlines.description: Alejandro Garnacho is struggling to stand out at Chelsea - so can the £40m signing from Manchester United still realise his potential at Stamford Bridge?

headlines.title: Union Berlin appoint first female head coach
headlines.description: Marie-Louise Eta becomes the first woman appointed to manage a men's team in one of Europe's top five leagues as she is named interim head coach of Bundesliga side Union Berlin.
